claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to understand a bit about your child or children.

**First, can you tell me:**
1. How many children do you have that you're claiming Child Benefit for?
2. What are their names and ages?

This will help me determine which eligibility path applies to your situation. The eligibility rules vary depending on whether a child is:
- Under 16 years old
- Between 16-19 years old (with additional education requirements)
- 20 or over (generally not eligible)

Please share this information and I'll guide you through the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who's 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for that information. Let me work through Alex's eligibility with you.

Since Alex is 8 years old, they fall into the "under 16" category. Now I need to ask you a few questions to complete the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 1: Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Since Alex is only 8 years old, I expect the answer is that they're not in paid work at all. Can you confirm this?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't work at all - they're only 8!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 2: Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

I need to confirm that Alex is not receiving benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in their own name. Again, given Alex is 8 years old, this would be very unusual, but I need to check. Can you confirm that Alex is not receiving any benefits in their own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 3: Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

Again, given that Alex is only 8 years old, I assume they are not in an apprenticeship program. Can you confirm this?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not - Alex is far too young for an apprenticeship!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 4: Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

To be eligible for Child Benefit, you need to meet one of the following residency conditions. The most common is simply that you normally live in the UK. 

**Do any of these apply to you:**
- You normally live in the UK
- You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You have pre-settled status and meet certain conditions (working, studying, sufficient resources, etc.)
- You're a Crown servant living abroad

Can you tell me about your residency status?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 5: Are you responsible for Alex?**

To be eligible, you need to meet at least one of these conditions:

1. **You live with Alex**, OR
2. **You contribute to Alex's upkeep** (paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit would provide for things like food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents), OR
3. **You pay maintenance** under a court order or agreement

Which of these applies to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me, but I do help out financially with things like clothes and school dinners.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. To determine if you meet the responsibility requirement through financial contributions, I need to understand a bit more.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. To qualify through financial contributions, you need to be contributing **at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment** towards Alex's upkeep. 

The government website has the current Child Benefit rates, but typically it's around £25 per week for the eldest or only child.

**Important questions:**

1. **Where does Alex live?** (Does Alex live with someone else, like their other parent?)

2. **How much do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep** (things like clothes, school dinners, etc.)? Is it at least around £25 per week or equivalent?

3. **Is anyone else already claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** This is crucial because only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any given time.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with their mum. I contribute about £26 a week towards their upkeep. As far as I know, their mum isn't claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing those details. This is important information.

You're contributing at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount (about £26 per week), which means you do meet the responsibility requirement. However, there's a key issue here:

**Since Alex lives with their mum, she would typically have the primary claim to Child Benefit.** Even if she's not currently claiming it, the rules prioritize the person the child lives with.

Let me continue through the assessment to check the other eligibility criteria, but I need to ask:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 6: About other claimants**

You mentioned that as far as you know, Alex's mum isn't claiming Child Benefit. However, I need to clarify:

1. **Are you certain that Alex's mum is NOT claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**
2. **If she's not claiming, have you discussed with her who should claim it?** 
3. **Would she be willing to let you claim it instead of claiming it herself?**

This is important because:
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at a time
- The person the child lives with usually has priority
- If both of you want to claim and there's a disagreement, HMRC would need to decide

Can you clarify the situation with Alex's mum?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm certain she's not claiming it currently. We haven't really discussed it formally, but she's not opposed to me claiming it since I'm contributing regularly to Alex's costs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex's mum is not currently claiming Child Benefit and she's not opposed to you claiming it, let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 7: About Alex's living situation**

I need to check if any special circumstances apply to Alex's living arrangements. Is Alex currently:

- **In hospital** (for an extended stay)?
- **In local authority care** (living in care arranged by the council)?
- **Fostered** (living with foster carers)?
- **Living normally** with their mum?

Which of these applies to Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is living normally with their mum.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question: Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the council paying for where Alex lives or for their care? Given that Alex lives with their mum in a normal family arrangement, I expect the answer is that the council is NOT paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance. Can you confirm this?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me navigate to the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.

### Why You're Eligible:

✅ **Alex qualifies** - At 8 years old, Alex is under 16 and meets all the child-related requirements  
✅ **You meet residency requirements** - You live in the UK (Manchester)  
✅ **You're responsible for Alex** - You contribute £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which meets the financial responsibility requirement  
✅ **No competing claim** - Alex's mum isn't currently claiming and doesn't oppose your claim  
✅ **No council payments** - The local council isn't paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance

### Important Note About Priority:

While you are eligible, please be aware that normally the person the child lives with (in this case, Alex's mum) would have priority to claim. However, since she's not claiming and is agreeable to you making the claim, you can proceed. **I would strongly recommend getting this agreement in writing or at least clearly documented,** as this could help avoid any confusion later.

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online** at www.gov.uk/child-benefit or **by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Charge** - If you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may need to pay back some or all of the benefit through your tax return
5. **Payments** are made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

**Reference:** https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Do you have any questions about the application process or anything else?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship - meeting all child-related criteria. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements. The claimant contributes approximately £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which meets the responsibility requirement even though Alex lives with their mother. Alex's mother is not currently claiming Child Benefit and is not opposed to the claimant making the claim. Alex is living in a normal family arrangement with no local council payments towards accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. While Alex lives with their mother, the claimant demonstrates financial responsibility by contributing at least the Child Benefit equivalent amount (£26/week) towards Alex's upkeep. Since no one else is currently claiming and there is no dispute, the claimant is eligible to apply.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements. The claimant contributes approximately £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which meets the responsibility requirement even though Alex lives with their mother. Alex's mother is not currently claiming Child Benefit and is not opposed to the claimant making the claim. Alex is living in a normal family arrangement with no local council payments towards accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.

Since no one else is currently claiming and there is no dispute, the claimant is eligible to apply.
